question: [File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/db/db.go -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: ConnectToDb()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the pool configuration (MaxIdleConnections, MaxOpenConnections) being set after the connection is established in ConnectToDb(), creating a race window where early record export queries use default pool settings and may be dropped?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/db/db.go -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: GetDbWithContext()] Can an unprivileged external user send requests with already-expired contexts to GetDbWithContext(), causing context.WithTimeout() to create an immediately-cancelled child context, so that all record export DB queries fail instantly and mirror nodes receive no records?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/db/db.go -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: ConnectToDb()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the absence of input validation on dbConfig fields passed to GetDsn() (host, port, username, dbname, password) to inject PostgreSQL connection string parameters that alter session behavior and cause incorrect records to be returned for mirror node exports?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/db/db.go -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: ConnectToDb()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that ConnectToDb() does not set a connection timeout (only a statement timeout), allowing the initial gorm.Open() to hang indefinitely during a network partition, blocking the mirror node from exporting any records?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/db/db.go -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: GetDbWithContext()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the statementTimeout being applied per-query (not per-transaction) in GetDbWithContext(), causing multi-statement record export transactions to be partially committed, resulting in inconsistent records exported to mirror nodes?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/db/db.go -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: ConnectToDb()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the sslmode=disable DSN parameter to perform a TCP session hijacking attack on the PostgreSQL connection, injecting false records into the mirror node export stream?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/db/db.go -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: ConnectToDb()] Can an unprivileged external user trigger a scenario where MaxIdleConnections is set to 0 (via misconfiguration reachable through API), causing every record export query to open and close a new connection, introducing latency that causes statement timeouts and missing records at mirror nodes?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/db/db.go -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: GetDbWithContext()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that GetDbWithContext() does not check whether the returned *gorm.DB is valid before returning it, so that callers receive a DB handle in an error state and silently export incorrect records to mirror nodes?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/db/db.go -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: ConnectToDb()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the absence of a circuit breaker in ConnectToDb() so that repeated DB failures cause the mirror node to continuously attempt and fail record exports, never falling back to a safe state or alerting operators?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/db/db.go -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: ConnectToDb()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that ConnectToDb() does not validate that the connected database is the correct one (no schema/version check), allowing a connection to a wrong or outdated database that returns incorrect records to mirror nodes?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/db/db.go -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: GetDbWithContext()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the time.Duration(d.statementTimeout)*time.Second multiplication in GetDbWithContext() where statementTimeout = 0 causes the noop path to be skipped incorrectly (off-by-one in the <= 0 check), leading to a zero-duration timeout that cancels all record export queries immediately?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/db/db.go -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: ConnectToDb()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that ConnectToDb() does not enforce read-only mode on the DB connection, allowing a compromised application layer to write incorrect data that is then exported to mirror nodes as legitimate records?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/db/db.go -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: ConnectToDb()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the pool's MaxLifetime setting (time.Duration(dbConfig.Pool.MaxLifetime) * time.Minute) to cause connections to expire during long-running record export batch operations, resulting in partial exports to mirror nodes?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/db/db.go -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: GetDbWithContext()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that GetDbWithContext() creates a new child context for every call without any rate limiting, allowing a flood of concurrent requests to create thousands of context.WithTimeout() goroutines that exhaust memory and cause record export failures?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/db/db.go -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: ConnectToDb()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the absence of connection string sanitization in GetDsn() to inject spaces or special characters in the password field that alter the libpq connection string parsing, causing ConnectToDb() to connect to an unintended database and export wrong records to mirror nodes?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/db/db.go -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: ConnectToDb()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that ConnectToDb() does not set a TCP keepalive or connection validation option, causing silent connection drops that result in record export queries returning empty results to mirror nodes without any error indication?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/db/db.go -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: GetDbWithContext()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the statementTimeout enforcement in GetDbWithContext() to cause a time-of-check-time-of-use (TOCTOU) race where a record is visible at query start but the timeout fires before the full result set is fetched, causing partial records to be exported to mirror nodes?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/db/db.go -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: ConnectToDb()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that ConnectToDb() logs the connection success with log.Info but does not log the DSN, making it impossible to detect if a wrong database (with incorrect records) was connected to for mirror node exports?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/db/db.go -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: ConnectToDb()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the absence of a maximum connection wait timeout in the pool configuration of ConnectToDb(), causing record export requests to block indefinitely when all connections are busy, resulting in missing records at mirror nodes?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/db/db.go -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: GetDbWithContext()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that GetDbWithContext() does not propagate cancellation errors back to the caller in a structured way, causing callers to treat a context-cancelled query (returning no rows) as a legitimate empty result and export missing records to mirror nodes?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/db/db.go -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: ConnectToDb()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that ConnectToDb() does not set PostgreSQL session-level parameters (e.g., search_path, timezone) on the connection, allowing a malicious database configuration to return records in an unexpected format that causes incorrect data to be exported to mirror nodes?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/db/db.go -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: ConnectToDb()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the pool's MaxOpenConnections limit in ConnectToDb() by holding open long-running API requests, starving legitimate record export queries and causing mirror nodes to receive incomplete block or transaction data?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/db/db.go -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: GetDbWithContext()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that GetDbWithContext() uses time.Duration(d.statementTimeout)*time.Second without overflow protection, where a large statementTimeout value wraps around to a negative duration, causing context.WithTimeout() to behave unexpectedly and drop record export queries?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/db/db.go -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: ConnectToDb()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that ConnectToDb() does not verify the PostgreSQL server certificate (sslmode=disable), allowing a rogue PostgreSQL server to serve fabricated records that are then exported to mirror nodes as authentic data?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/db/db.go -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: ConnectToDb()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the absence of connection pool pre-warming in ConnectToDb(), causing the first burst of record export requests after startup to fail due to connection establishment latency, resulting in missing records at mirror nodes during the startup window?
